ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Drapion V'; PokemonTCG.io confirms nationalPokedexNumbers: [452]; art depicts a large purple scorpion-type creature with prominent claws, matching Drapion's canonical design exactly. Internal Pokemon IP, no external crossover risk.

trivia_bullets.text: Drapion is National Pokedex no. 452, a dual Poison/Dark type introduced in Generation IV (Diamond, Pearl, Platinum, 2006). It evolves from Skorupi at level 40. Notably, Skorupi is Poison/Bug type, but Drapion loses the Bug typing upon evolution — a rare type-loss on evolution. [Bulbapedia: Drapion, via WebSearch snippet]

Hazardous Claws (4 energy, 130 damage) inflicts both Paralyzed and Poisoned on the opponent's Active Pokemon while discarding 2 Energy from Drapion V — a high-cost double-status attack that mirrors Drapion's in-game Battle Armor / Sniper abilities focused on sustained threat. The V-rule means an opponent takes 2 Prize cards when Drapion V is knocked out, raising the stakes of its aggressive role. [PokemonTCG.io: swsh4-106 oracle text]

Illustrated by Eske Yoshinob, a frequent contributor to Sword & Shield era V-rank cards. Drapion V has no VMAX or VSTAR counterpart in the VIV set — it is a standalone V, making it a single-step threat without an evolution line within the V rank framework. [PokemonTCG.io: swsh4-106 metadata]
